zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Bourchier於1490年出生在英國艾可時，她的父親John Bourchier24歲，母親Katherine Howard23歲。她於大約1515年與Sir Edmund II Knyvett在英國諾福爾可阿申維爾索普結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1562年2月17日在英國諾福爾可阿申維爾索普享年72歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可阿申維爾索普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Bourchier于1490年出生在英国艾可时，她的父亲John Bourchier24岁，母亲Katherine Howard23岁。她于大约1515年与Sir Edmund II Knyvett在英国諾福爾可阿申維爾索普结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1562年2月17日在英国諾福爾可阿申維爾索普享年72岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可阿申維爾索普。

nameMeanings.name: Bourchier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: perhaps a variant of   Burcher   (  see  Bircher  ). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2). The name has sometimes been altered to or confused with   Bouchier   and   Boucher  .

2 Norman, English: locative name of uncertain origin but it is presumably from a French place-name, perhaps Boursières in Haute-Saône. The name was early confused with other surnames and may survive in Bouchier, Bowcher, Bowser, and Boozer . The surname gave rise to a number of minor place-names in Essex: Bourchier’s alias Bouchier’s Hall in Aldham, Bourchier’s Hall in Messing; Bouchier’s Grange in Great Coggeshall, Bouchiers Chapel in Tollesbury, Boarstye Fm and Bowser’s Hall in Rivenhall (Essex), and Boose’s Green in Colne Engayne.

variants.name: Bourcier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Lucien, Armand, Emile.
French: variant of   Boursier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The (majority of the) American bearers of the surname Bourcier are descendants of Jean Boursier dit Lavigne from France (see Boursier ).

variants.name: Bircher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Albrecht, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
South German and Swiss German: topographic name for someone who lived by a birch tree or in a birch wood, from Middle High German birche ‘birch’ + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
English: habitational name from Birchover in Derbyshire or Bircher in Hereford, both named as from Old English birce ‘birch’ + ofer ‘ridge’.

variants.name: Bouchier
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Butcher  .
2  English: alternatively a variant of   Belcher  .
3  Norman, English: possibly a variant of   Bourchier   (2).

variants.name: Bowser
meanings: English: nickname from either from Old French beu cher ‘good company’, or from Old French beu sire ‘fair sir’ (see Belcher ), given either to a fine gentleman (perhaps ironically), or to someone who made frequent use of this term of address. Compare Bonser and Boozer .
English: variant of   Bolsover  .
Americanized form of German   Bauser  .

variants.name: Boozer
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German   Buser  .
English (Kent): variant of   Bowser   1.

variants.name: Burcher
meanings: English: variant of   Bircher  .

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Borger
meanings: North German (also Börger) and Dutch: variant of Burger .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname from Yiddish borger ‘borrower’ or ‘lender’.
Norwegian and Swedish: habitational name for someone from a place called Borg (see   Borg  ), or a topographic name for someone living by a fortification or near a hill resembling a fortification.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Bourchier was born in 1490, in Essex, England, her father, John Bourchier, was 24 and her mother, Katherine Howard, was 23. She married Sir Edmund II Knyvett about 1515, in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 17 February 1562, in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, at the age of 72, and was buried in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Bourchier est née en 1490, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, John Bourchier, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Katherine Howard, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Edmund II Knyvett environ 1515, à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 février 1562, à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Bourchier 1490 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Bourchier 24 und ihre Mutter Katherine Howard 23. Sie hat um 1515 in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England Sir Edmund II Knyvett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. Februar 1562 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England und wurde in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Bourchier è nata il nel 1490 nel Essex, England, sua padre, John Bourchier, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Katherine Howard, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Sir Edmund II Knyvett circa 1515, nel Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 17 febbraio 1562, nel Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolta a Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Bourchierが1490年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父John Bourchierは24歳，母Katherine Howardは23歳であった。約1515年にイギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペでSir Edmund II Knyvettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1562年2月17日にイギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペで72歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Bourchier(이)가 1490년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Bourchier(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Howard(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1515년 영국 노르폴크 애슈웰소에서 Sir Edmund II Knyvett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1562년 2월 17일 영국 노르폴크 애슈웰소에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 애슈웰소에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Bourchier nasceu em 1490, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Bourchier, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Howard, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Edmund II Knyvett aproximadamente 1515, em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de fevereiro de 1562, em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 72 anos, e foi sepultada em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Bourchier родилась в 1490 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени John Bourchier было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Katherine Howard, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edmund II Knyvett около 1515 г. в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 17 февраля 1562 г. в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Bourchier nació en 1490, en Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, John Bourchier, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Katherine Howard, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Sir Edmund II Knyvett aproximadamente 1515, en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 17 de febrero de 1562, en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultada en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
